verses.sanskrit: मैत्रेय उवाचमनोस्तु शतरूपायां तिस्र: कन्याश्च जज्ञिरे ।आकूतिर्देवहूतिश्च प्रसूतिरिति विश्रुता: ॥ १ ॥
verses.transliteration: maitreya uvācamanos tu śatarūpāyāṁtisraḥ kanyāś ca jajñireākūtir devahūtiś caprasūtir iti viśrutāḥ
verses.text: Śrī Maitreya said: Svāyambhuva Manu begot three daughters in his wife, Śatarūpā, and their names were Ākūti, Devahūti and Prasūti.

verses.sanskrit: आकूतिं रुचये प्रादादपि भ्रातृमतीं नृप: ।पुत्रिकाधर्ममाश्रित्य शतरूपानुमोदित: ॥ २ ॥
verses.transliteration: ākūtiṁ rucaye prādādapi bhrātṛmatīṁ nṛpaḥputrikā-dharmam āśrityaśatarūpānumoditaḥ
verses.text: Ākūti had two brothers, but in spite of her brothers, King Svāyambhuva Manu handed her over to Prajāpati Ruci on the condition that the son born of her be returned to Manu as his son. This he did in consultation with his wife, Śatarūpā.

verses.sanskrit: प्रजापति: स भगवान् रुचिस्तस्यामजीजनत् ।मिथुनं ब्रह्मवर्चस्वी परमेण समाधिना ॥ ३ ॥
verses.transliteration: prajāpatiḥ sa bhagavānrucis tasyām ajījanatmithunaṁ brahma-varcasvīparameṇa samādhinā
verses.text: Ruci, who was very powerful in his brahminical qualifications and was appointed one of the progenitors of the living entities, begot one son and one daughter by his wife, Ākūti.

verses.sanskrit: यस्तयो: पुरुष: साक्षाद्विष्णुर्यज्ञस्वरूपधृक् ।या स्त्री सा दक्षिणा भूतेरंशभूतानपायिनी ॥ ४ ॥
verses.transliteration: yas tayoḥ puruṣaḥ sākṣādviṣṇur yajña-svarūpa-dhṛkyā strī sā dakṣiṇā bhūteraṁśa-bhūtānapāyinī
verses.text: Of the two children born of Ākūti, the male child was directly an incarnation of the Supreme Personality of Godhead, and His name was Yajña, which is another name of Lord Viṣṇu. The female child was a partial incarnation of Lakṣmī, the goddess of fortune, the eternal consort of Lord Viṣṇu.

verses.sanskrit: आनिन्ये स्वगृहं पुत्र्या: पुत्रं विततरोचिषम् ।स्वायम्भुवो मुदा युक्तो रुचिर्जग्राह दक्षिणाम् ॥ ५ ॥
verses.transliteration: āninye sva-gṛhaṁ putryāḥputraṁ vitata-rociṣamsvāyambhuvo mudā yuktorucir jagrāha dakṣiṇām
verses.text: Svāyambhuva Manu very gladly brought home the beautiful boy named Yajña, and Ruci, his son-in-law, kept with him the daughter, Dakṣiṇā.

verses.sanskrit: तां कामयानां भगवानुवाह यजुषां पति: ।तुष्टायां तोषमापन्नोऽजनयद् द्वादशात्मजान् ॥ ६ ॥
verses.transliteration: tāṁ kāmayānāṁ bhagavānuvāha yajuṣāṁ patiḥtuṣṭāyāṁ toṣam āpanno’janayad dvādaśātmajān
verses.text: The Lord of the ritualistic performance of yajña later married Dakṣiṇā, who was anxious to have the Personality of Godhead as her husband, and in this wife the Lord was also very much pleased to beget twelve children.

verses.sanskrit: तोष: प्रतोष: सन्तोषो भद्र: शान्तिरिडस्पति: ।इध्म: कविर्विभु: स्वह्न: सुदेवो रोचनो द्विषट् ॥ ७ ॥
verses.transliteration: toṣaḥ pratoṣaḥ santoṣobhadraḥ śāntir iḍaspatiḥidhmaḥ kavir vibhuḥ svahnaḥsudevo rocano dvi-ṣaṭ
verses.text: The twelve boys born of Yajña and Dakṣiṇā were named Toṣa, Pratoṣa, Santoṣa, Bhadra, Śānti, Iḍaspati, Idhma, Kavi, Vibhu, Svahna, Sudeva and Rocana.

verses.sanskrit: तुषिता नाम ते देवा आसन्स्वायम्भुवान्तरे ।मरीचिमिश्रा ऋषयो यज्ञ: सुरगणेश्वर: ॥ ८ ॥
verses.transliteration: tuṣitā nāma te devāāsan svāyambhuvāntaremarīci-miśrā ṛṣayoyajñaḥ sura-gaṇeśvaraḥ
verses.text: During the time of Svāyambhuva Manu, these sons all became the demigods collectively named the Tuṣitas. Marīci became the head of the seven ṛṣis, and Yajña became the king of the demigods, Indra.

verses.sanskrit: प्रियव्रतोत्तानपादौ मनुपुत्रौ महौजसौ ।तत्पुत्रपौत्रनप्तृणामनुवृत्तं तदन्तरम् ॥ ९ ॥
verses.transliteration: priyavratottānapādaumanu-putrau mahaujasautat-putra-pautra-naptṝṇāmanuvṛttaṁ tad-antaram
verses.text: Svāyambhuva Manu’s two sons, Priyavrata and Uttānapāda, became very powerful kings, and their sons and grandsons spread all over the three worlds during that period.

verses.sanskrit: देवहूतिमदात्तात कर्दमायात्मजां मनु: ।तत्सम्बन्धि श्रुतप्रायं भवता गदतो मम ॥ १० ॥
verses.transliteration: devahūtim adāt tātakardamāyātmajāṁ manuḥtat-sambandhi śruta-prāyaṁbhavatā gadato mama
verses.text: My dear son, Svāyambhuva Manu handed over his very dear daughter Devahūti to Kardama Muni. I have already spoken to you about them, and you have heard about them almost in full.

verses.sanskrit: दक्षाय ब्रह्मपुत्राय प्रसूतिं भगवान्मनु: ।प्रायच्छद्यत्कृत: सर्गस्त्रिलोक्यां विततो महान् ॥ ११ ॥
verses.transliteration: dakṣāya brahma-putrāyaprasūtiṁ bhagavān manuḥprāyacchad yat-kṛtaḥ sargastri-lokyāṁ vitato mahān
verses.text: Svāyambhuva Manu handed over his daughter Prasūti to the son of Brahmā named Dakṣa, who was also one of the progenitors of the living entities. The descendants of Dakṣa are spread throughout the three worlds.

verses.sanskrit: या: कर्दमसुता: प्रोक्ता नव ब्रह्मर्षिपत्नय: ।तासां प्रसूतिप्रसवं प्रोच्यमानं निबोध मे ॥ १२ ॥
verses.transliteration: yāḥ kardama-sutāḥ proktānava brahmarṣi-patnayaḥtāsāṁ prasūti-prasavaṁprocyamānaṁ nibodha me
verses.text: You have already been informed about the nine daughters of Kardama Muni, who were handed over to nine different sages. I shall now describe the descendants of those nine daughters. Please hear from me.

verses.sanskrit: पत्नी मरीचेस्तु कला सुषुवे कर्दमात्मजा ।कश्यपं पूर्णिमानं च ययोरापूरितं जगत् ॥ १३ ॥
verses.transliteration: patnī marīces tu kalāsuṣuve kardamātmajākaśyapaṁ pūrṇimānaṁ cayayor āpūritaṁ jagat
verses.text: Kardama Muni’s daughter Kalā, who was married to Marīci, gave birth to two children, whose names were Kaśyapa and Pūrṇimā. Their descendants are spread all over the world.

verses.sanskrit: पूर्णिमासूत विरजं विश्वगं च परन्तप ।देवकुल्यां हरे: पादशौचाद्याभूत्सरिद्दिव: ॥ १४ ॥
verses.transliteration: pūrṇimāsūta virajaṁviśvagaṁ ca parantapadevakulyāṁ hareḥ pāda-śaucād yābhūt sarid divaḥ
verses.text: My dear Vidura, of the two sons, Kaśyapa and Pūrṇimā, Pūrṇimā begot three children, namely Viraja, Viśvaga and Devakulyā. Of these three, Devakulyā was the water which washed the lotus feet of the Personality of Godhead and which later on transformed into the Ganges of the heavenly planets.

verses.sanskrit: अत्रे: पत्‍न्यनसूया त्रीञ्जज्ञे सुयशस: सुतान् ।दत्तं दुर्वाससं सोममात्मेशब्रह्मसम्भवान् ॥ १५ ॥
verses.transliteration: atreḥ patny anasūyā trīñjajñe suyaśasaḥ sutāndattaṁ durvāsasaṁ somamātmeśa-brahma-sambhavān
verses.text: Anasūyā, the wife of Atri Muni, gave birth to three very famous sons — Soma, Dattātreya and Durvāsā — who were partial representations of Lord Viṣṇu, Lord Śiva and Lord Brahmā. Soma was a partial representation of Lord Brahmā, Dattātreya was a partial representation of Lord Viṣṇu, and Durvāsā was a partial representation of Lord Śiva.

verses.sanskrit: विदुर उवाचअत्रेर्गृहे सुरश्रेष्ठा: स्थित्युत्पत्त्यन्तहेतव: ।किञ्चिच्चिकीर्षवो जाता एतदाख्याहि मे गुरो ॥ १६ ॥
verses.transliteration: vidura uvācaatrer gṛhe sura-śreṣṭhāḥsthity-utpatty-anta-hetavaḥkiñcic cikīrṣavo jātāetad ākhyāhi me guro
verses.text: After hearing this, Vidura inquired from Maitreya: My dear master, how is it that the three deities Brahmā, Viṣṇu and Śiva, who are the creator, maintainer and destroyer of the whole creation, became the offspring of the wife of Atri Muni?

verses.sanskrit: मैत्रेय उवाचब्रह्मणा चोदित: सृष्टावत्रिर्ब्रह्मविदां वर: ।सह पत्‍न्या ययावृक्षं कुलाद्रिं तपसि स्थित: ॥ १७ ॥
verses.transliteration: maitreya uvācabrahmaṇā coditaḥ sṛṣṭāvatrir brahma-vidāṁ varaḥsaha patnyā yayāv ṛkṣaṁkulādriṁ tapasi sthitaḥ
verses.text: Maitreya said: When Lord Brahmā ordered Atri Muni to create generations after marrying Anasūyā, Atri Muni and his wife went to perform severe austerities in the valley of the mountain known as Ṛkṣa.

verses.sanskrit: तस्मिन् प्रसूनस्तबकपलाशाशोककानने ।वार्भि: स्रवद्‌भिरुद्‍घुष्टेनिर्विन्ध्याया: समन्तत: ॥ १८ ॥
verses.transliteration: tasmin prasūna-stabaka-palāśāśoka-kānanevārbhiḥ sravadbhir udghuṣṭenirvindhyāyāḥ samantataḥ
verses.text: In that mountain valley flows a river named Nirvindhyā. On the bank of the river are many aśoka trees and other plants full of palāśa flowers, and there is always the sweet sound of water flowing from a waterfall. The husband and wife reached that beautiful place.

verses.sanskrit: प्राणायामेन संयम्य मनो वर्षशतं मुनि: ।अतिष्ठदेकपादेन निर्द्वन्द्वोऽनिलभोजन: ॥ १९ ॥
verses.transliteration: prāṇāyāmena saṁyamyamano varṣa-śataṁ muniḥatiṣṭhad eka-pādenanirdvandvo ’nila-bhojanaḥ
verses.text: There the great sage concentrated his mind by the yogic breathing exercises, and thereby controlling all attachment, he remained standing on one leg only, eating nothing but air, and stood there on one leg for one hundred years.

verses.sanskrit: शरणं तं प्रपद्येऽहं य एव जगदीश्वर: ।प्रजामात्मसमां मह्यं प्रयच्छत्विति चिन्तयन् ॥ २० ॥
verses.transliteration: śaraṇaṁ taṁ prapadye ’haṁya eva jagad-īśvaraḥprajām ātma-samāṁ mahyaṁprayacchatv iti cintayan
verses.text: He was thinking: May the Lord of the universe, of whom I have taken shelter, kindly be pleased to offer me a son exactly like Him.

verses.sanskrit: तप्यमानं त्रिभुवनं प्राणायामैधसाग्निना ।निर्गतेन मुनेर्मूर्ध्न: समीक्ष्य प्रभवस्त्रय: ॥ २१ ॥
verses.transliteration: tapyamānaṁ tri-bhuvanaṁprāṇāyāmaidhasāgninānirgatena muner mūrdhnaḥsamīkṣya prabhavas trayaḥ
verses.text: While Atri Muni was engaged in these severe austerities, a blazing fire came out of his head by virtue of his breathing exercise, and that fire was seen by the three principal deities of the three worlds.

verses.sanskrit: अप्सरोमुनिगन्धर्वसिद्धविद्याधरोरगै: ।वितायमानयशसस्तदाश्रमपदं ययु: ॥ २२ ॥
verses.transliteration: apsaro-muni-gandharva-siddha-vidyādharoragaiḥvitāyamāna-yaśasastad-āśrama-padaṁ yayuḥ
verses.text: At that time, the three deities approached the hermitage of Atri Muni, accompanied by the denizens of the heavenly planets, such as the celestial beauties, the Gandharvas, the Siddhas, the Vidyādharas and the Nāgas. Thus they entered the āśrama of the great sage, who had become famous by his austerities.

verses.sanskrit: तत्प्रादुर्भावसंयोगविद्योतितमना मुनि: ।उत्तिष्ठन्नेकपादेन ददर्श विबुधर्षभान् ॥ २३ ॥
verses.transliteration: tat-prādurbhāva-saṁyoga-vidyotita-manā muniḥuttiṣṭhann eka-pādenadadarśa vibudharṣabhān
verses.text: The sage was standing on one leg, but as soon as he saw that the three deities had appeared before him, he was so pleased to see them all together that despite great difficulty he approached them on one leg.

verses.sanskrit: प्रणम्य दण्डवद्भूमावुपतस्थेऽर्हणाञ्जलि: ।वृषहंससुपर्णस्थान् स्वै: स्वैश्चिह्नैश्च चिह्नितान् ॥ २४ ॥
verses.transliteration: praṇamya daṇḍavad bhūmāvupatasthe ’rhaṇāñjaliḥvṛṣa-haṁsa-suparṇa-sthānsvaiḥ svaiś cihnaiś ca cihnitān
verses.text: Thereafter he began to offer prayers to the three deities, who were seated on different carriers — a bull, a swan and Garuḍa — and who held in their hands a drum, kuśa grass and a discus. The sage offered them his respects by falling down like a stick.

verses.sanskrit: कृपावलोकेन हसद्वदनेनोपलम्भितान् ।तद्रोचिषा प्रतिहते निमील्य मुनिरक्षिणी ॥ २५ ॥
verses.transliteration: kṛpāvalokena hasad-vadanenopalambhitāntad-rociṣā pratihatenimīlya munir akṣiṇī
verses.text: Atri Muni was greatly pleased to see that the three devas were gracious towards him. His eyes were dazzled by the effulgence of their bodies, and therefore he closed his eyes for the time being.

verses.sanskrit: चेतस्तत्प्रवणं युञ्जन्नस्तावीत्संहताञ्जलि: ।श्लक्ष्णया सूक्तया वाचा सर्वलोकगरीयस: ॥ २६ ॥अत्रिरुवाचविश्वोद्भवस्थितिलयेषु विभज्यमानै-र्मायागुणैरनुयुगं विगृहीतदेहा: । ते ब्रह्मविष्णुगिरिशा: प्रणतोऽस्म्यहं व-स्तेभ्य: क एव भवतां म इहोपहूत: ॥ २७ ॥
verses.transliteration: cetas tat-pravaṇaṁ yuñjannastāvīt saṁhatāñjaliḥślakṣṇayā sūktayā vācāsarva-loka-garīyasaḥ
verses.text: But since his heart was already attracted by the deities, somehow or other he gathered his senses, and with folded hands and sweet words he began to offer prayers to the predominating deities of the universe. The great sage Atri said: O Lord Brahmā, Lord Viṣṇu and Lord Śiva, you have divided yourself into three bodies by accepting the three modes of material nature, as you do in every millennium for the creation, maintenance and dissolution of the cosmic manifestation. I offer my respectful obeisances unto all of you and beg to inquire whom of you three I have called by my prayer.

verses.sanskrit: एको मयेह भगवान्विविधप्रधानै-श्चित्तीकृत: प्रजननाय कथं नु यूयम् ।अत्रागतास्तनुभृतां मनसोऽपि दूराद्ब्रूत प्रसीदत महानिह विस्मयो मे ॥ २८ ॥
verses.transliteration: eko mayeha bhagavān vividha-pradhānaiścittī-kṛtaḥ prajananāya kathaṁ nu yūyamatrāgatās tanu-bhṛtāṁ manaso ’pi dūrādbrūta prasīdata mahān iha vismayo me
verses.text: I called for the Supreme Personality of Godhead, desiring a son like Him, and I thought of Him only. But although He is far beyond the mental speculation of man, all three of you have come here. Kindly let me know how you have come, for I am greatly bewildered about this.

verses.sanskrit: मैत्रेय उवाचइति तस्य वच: श्रुत्वा त्रयस्ते विबुधर्षभा: ।प्रत्याहु: श्लक्ष्णया वाचा प्रहस्य तमृषिं प्रभो ॥ २९ ॥
verses.transliteration: maitreya uvācaiti tasya vacaḥ śrutvātrayas te vibudharṣabhāḥpratyāhuḥ ślakṣṇayā vācāprahasya tam ṛṣiṁ prabho
verses.text: The great sage Maitreya continued: Upon hearing Atri Muni speak in that way, the three great deities smiled, and they replied in the following sweet words.

verses.sanskrit: देवा ऊचु:यथा कृतस्ते सङ्कल्पो भाव्यं तेनैव नान्यथा ।सत्सङ्कल्पस्य ते ब्रह्मन् यद्वै ध्यायति ते वयम् ॥ ३० ॥
verses.transliteration: devā ūcuḥyathā kṛtas te saṅkalpobhāvyaṁ tenaiva nānyathāsat-saṅkalpasya te brahmanyad vai dhyāyati te vayam
verses.text: The three deities told Atri Muni: Dear brāhmaṇa, you are perfect in your determination, and therefore as you have decided, so it will happen; it will not happen otherwise. We are all the same person upon whom you were meditating, and therefore we have all come to you.

verses.sanskrit: अथास्मदंशभूतास्ते आत्मजा लोकविश्रुता: ।भवितारोऽङ्ग भद्रं ते विस्रप्स्यन्ति च ते यश: ॥ ३१ ॥
verses.transliteration: athāsmad-aṁśa-bhūtās teātmajā loka-viśrutāḥbhavitāro ’ṅga bhadraṁ tevisrapsyanti ca te yaśaḥ
verses.text: You will have sons who will represent a partial manifestation of our potency, and because we desire all good fortune for you, those sons will glorify your reputation throughout the world.

verses.sanskrit: एवं कामवरं दत्त्वा प्रतिजग्मु: सुरेश्वरा: ।सभाजितास्तयो: सम्यग्दम्पत्योर्मिषतोस्तत: ॥ ३२ ॥
verses.transliteration: evaṁ kāma-varaṁ dattvāpratijagmuḥ sureśvarāḥsabhājitās tayoḥ samyagdampatyor miṣatos tataḥ
verses.text: Thus, while the couple looked on, the three deities Brahmā, Viṣṇu and Maheśvara disappeared from that place after bestowing upon Atri Muni the benediction.

verses.sanskrit: सोमोऽभूद्ब्रह्मणोंऽशेन दत्तो विष्णोस्तु योगवित् ।दुर्वासा: शङ्करस्यांशो निबोधाङ्गिरस: प्रजा: ॥ ३३ ॥
verses.transliteration: somo ’bhūd brahmaṇo ’ṁśenadatto viṣṇos tu yogavitdurvāsāḥ śaṅkarasyāṁśonibodhāṅgirasaḥ prajāḥ
verses.text: Thereafter, from the partial representation of Brahmā, the moon-god was born of them; from the partial representation of Viṣṇu, the great mystic Dattātreya was born; and from the partial representation of Śaṅkara [Lord Śiva], Durvāsā was born. Now you may hear from me of the many sons of Aṅgirā.

verses.sanskrit: श्रद्धा त्वङ्गिरस: पत्नी चतस्रोऽसूत कन्यका: ।सिनीवाली कुहू राका चतुर्थ्यनुमतिस्तथा ॥ ३४ ॥
verses.transliteration: śraddhā tv aṅgirasaḥ patnīcatasro ’sūta kanyakāḥsinīvālī kuhū rākācaturthy anumatis tathā
verses.text: Aṅgirā’s wife, Śraddhā, gave birth to four daughters, named Sinīvālī, Kuhū, Rākā and Anumati.

verses.sanskrit: तत्पुत्रावपरावास्तां ख्यातौ स्वारोचिषेऽन्तरे ।उतथ्यो भगवान्साक्षाद् ब्रह्मिष्ठश्च बृहस्पति: ॥ ३५ ॥
verses.transliteration: tat-putrāv aparāv āstāṁkhyātau svārociṣe ’ntareutathyo bhagavān sākṣādbrahmiṣṭhaś ca bṛhaspatiḥ
verses.text: Besides these four daughters, she also had another two sons. One of them was known as Utathya, and the other was the learned scholar Bṛhaspati.

verses.sanskrit: पुलस्त्योऽजनयत्पत्‍न्यामगस्त्यं च हविर्भुवि ।सोऽन्यजन्मनि दह्राग्निर्विश्रवाश्च महातपा: ॥ ३६ ॥
verses.transliteration: pulastyo ’janayat patnyāmagastyaṁ ca havirbhuviso ’nya-janmani dahrāgnirviśravāś ca mahā-tapāḥ
verses.text: Pulastya begot in his wife, Havirbhū, one son of the name Agastya, who in his next birth became Dahrāgni. Besides him, Pulastya begot another very great and saintly son, whose name was Viśravā.

verses.sanskrit: तस्य यक्षपतिर्देव: कुबेरस्त्विडविडासुत: ।रावण: कुम्भकर्णश्च तथान्यस्यां विभीषण: ॥ ३७ ॥
verses.transliteration: tasya yakṣa-patir devaḥkuberas tv iḍaviḍā-sutaḥrāvaṇaḥ kumbhakarṇaś catathānyasyāṁ vibhīṣaṇaḥ
verses.text: Viśravā had two wives. The first wife was Iḍaviḍā, from whom Kuvera, the master of all Yakṣas, was born, and the next wife was named Keśinī, from whom three sons were born — Rāvaṇa, Kumbhakarṇa and Vibhīṣaṇa.

verses.sanskrit: पुलहस्य गतिर्भार्या त्रीनसूत सती सुतान् ।कर्मश्रेष्ठं वरीयांसं सहिष्णुं च महामते ॥ ३८ ॥
verses.transliteration: pulahasya gatir bhāryātrīn asūta satī sutānkarmaśreṣṭhaṁ varīyāṁsaṁsahiṣṇuṁ ca mahā-mate
verses.text: Gati, the wife of the sage Pulaha, gave birth to three sons, named Karmaśreṣṭha, Varīyān and Sahiṣṇu, and all of them were great sages.

verses.sanskrit: क्रतोरपि क्रिया भार्या वालखिल्यानसूयत ।ऋषीन्षष्टिसहस्राणि ज्वलतो ब्रह्मतेजसा ॥ ३९ ॥
verses.transliteration: krator api kriyā bhāryāvālakhilyān asūyataṛṣīn ṣaṣṭi-sahasrāṇijvalato brahma-tejasā
verses.text: Kratu’s wife, Kriyā, gave birth to sixty thousand great sages, named the Vālakhilyas. All these sages were greatly advanced in spiritual knowledge, and their bodies were illuminated by such knowledge.

verses.sanskrit: ऊर्जायां जज्ञिरे पुत्रा वसिष्ठस्य परन्तप ।चित्रकेतुप्रधानास्ते सप्त ब्रह्मर्षयोऽमला: ॥ ४० ॥
verses.transliteration: ūrjāyāṁ jajñire putrāvasiṣṭhasya parantapacitraketu-pradhānās tesapta brahmarṣayo ’malāḥ
verses.text: The great sage Vasiṣṭha begot in his wife, Ūrjā [sometimes called Arundhatī], seven spotlessly great sages, headed by the sage named Citraketu.

verses.sanskrit: चित्रकेतु: सुरोचिश्च विरजा मित्र एव च ।उल्बणो वसुभृद्यानो द्युमान्शक्त्यादयोऽपरे ॥ ४१ ॥
verses.transliteration: citraketuḥ surociś cavirajā mitra eva caulbaṇo vasubhṛdyānodyumān śakty-ādayo ’pare
verses.text: The names of these seven sages are as follows: Citraketu, Suroci, Virajā, Mitra, Ulbaṇa, Vasubhṛdyāna and Dyumān. Some other very competent sons were born from Vasiṣṭha’s other wife.

verses.sanskrit: चित्तिस्त्वथर्वण: पत्नी लेभे पुत्रं धृतव्रतम् ।दध्यञ्चमश्वशिरसं भृगोर्वंशं निबोध मे ॥ ४२ ॥
verses.transliteration: cittis tv atharvaṇaḥ patnīlebhe putraṁ dhṛta-vratamdadhyañcam aśvaśirasaṁbhṛgor vaṁśaṁ nibodha me
verses.text: Citti, wife of the sage Atharvā, gave birth to a son named Aśvaśirā by accepting a great vow called Dadhyañca. Now you may hear from me about the descendants of the sage Bhṛgu.

verses.sanskrit: भृगु: ख्यात्यां महाभाग: पत्‍न्यां पुत्रानजीजनत् ।धातारं च विधातारं श्रियं च भगवत्पराम् ॥ ४३ ॥
verses.transliteration: bhṛguḥ khyātyāṁ mahā-bhāgaḥpatnyāṁ putrān ajījanatdhātāraṁ ca vidhātāraṁśriyaṁ ca bhagavat-parām
verses.text: The sage Bhṛgu was highly fortunate. In his wife, known as Khyāti, he begot two sons, named Dhātā and Vidhātā, and one daughter, named Śrī, who was very much devoted to the Supreme Personality of Godhead.

verses.sanskrit: आयतिं नियतिं चैव सुते मेरुस्तयोरदात् ।ताभ्यां तयोरभवतां मृकण्ड: प्राण एव च ॥ ४४ ॥
verses.transliteration: āyatiṁ niyatiṁ caivasute merus tayor adāttābhyāṁ tayor abhavatāṁmṛkaṇḍaḥ prāṇa eva ca
verses.text: The sage Meru had two daughters, named Āyati and Niyati, whom he gave in charity to Dhātā and Vidhātā. Āyati and Niyati gave birth to two sons, Mṛkaṇḍa and Prāṇa.

verses.sanskrit: मार्कण्डेयो मृकण्डस्य प्राणाद्वेदशिरा मुनि: ।कविश्च भार्गवो यस्य भगवानुशना सुत: ॥ ४५ ॥
verses.transliteration: mārkaṇḍeyo mṛkaṇḍasyaprāṇād vedaśirā muniḥkaviś ca bhārgavo yasyabhagavān uśanā sutaḥ
verses.text: From Mṛkaṇḍa, Mārkaṇḍeya Muni was born, and from Prāṇa, the sage Vedaśirā, whose son was Uśanā [Śukrācārya], also known as Kavi. Thus Kavi also belonged to the descendants of the Bhṛgu dynasty.

verses.sanskrit: त एते मुनय: क्षत्तर्लोकान्सर्गैरभावयन् ।एष कर्दमदौहित्रसन्तान: कथितस्तव ॥ ४६ ॥श‍ृण्वत: श्रद्दधानस्य सद्य: पापहर: पर: ।प्रसूतिं मानवीं दक्ष उपयेमे ह्यजात्मज: ॥ ४७ ॥
verses.transliteration: ta ete munayaḥ kṣattarlokān sargair abhāvayaneṣa kardama-dauhitra-santānaḥ kathitas tava
verses.text: My dear Vidura, the population of the universe was thus increased by the descendants of these sages and the daughters of Kardama. Anyone who hears the descriptions of this dynasty with faith will be relieved from all sinful reactions. Another of Manu’s daughters, known as Prasūti, married the son of Brahmā named Dakṣa.

verses.sanskrit: तस्यां ससर्ज दुहितृ: षोडशामललोचना: ।त्रयोदशादाद्धर्माय तथैकामग्नये विभु: ॥ ४८ ॥
verses.transliteration: tasyāṁ sasarja duhitṝḥṣoḍaśāmala-locanāḥtrayodaśādād dharmāyatathaikām agnaye vibhuḥ
verses.text: Dakṣa begot sixteen very beautiful daughters with lotuslike eyes in his wife Prasūti. Of these sixteen daughters, thirteen were given in marriage to Dharma, and one daughter was given to Agni.

verses.sanskrit: पितृभ्य एकां युक्तेभ्यो भवायैकां भवच्छिदे ।श्रद्धा मैत्री दया शान्तिस्तुष्टि: पुष्टि: क्रियोन्नति: ॥ ४९ ॥बुद्धिर्मेधा तितिक्षा ह्रीर्मूर्तिर्धर्मस्य पत्नय: ।श्रद्धासूत शुभं मैत्री प्रसादमभयं दया ॥ ५० ॥शान्ति: सुखं मुदं तुष्टि: स्मयं पुष्टिरसूयत ।योगं क्रियोन्नतिर्दर्पमर्थं बुद्धिरसूयत ॥ ५१ ॥मेधा स्मृतिं तितिक्षा तु क्षेमं ह्री: प्रश्रयं सुतम् ।मूर्ति: सर्वगुणोत्पत्तिर्नरनारायणावृषी ॥ ५२ ॥
verses.transliteration: pitṛbhya ekāṁ yuktebhyobhavāyaikāṁ bhava-cchideśraddhā maitrī dayā śāntistuṣṭiḥ puṣṭiḥ kriyonnatiḥ
verses.text: One of the remaining two daughters was given in charity to the Pitṛloka, where she resides very amicably, and the other was given to Lord Śiva, who is the deliverer of sinful persons from material entanglement. The names of the thirteen daughters of Dakṣa who were given to Dharma are Śraddhā, Maitrī, Dayā, Śānti, Tuṣṭi, Puṣṭi, Kriyā, Unnati, Buddhi, Medhā, Titikṣā, Hrī and Mūrti. These thirteen daughters produced the following sons: Śraddhā gave birth to Śubha, Maitrī produced Prasāda, Dayā gave birth to Abhaya, Śānti gave birth to Sukha, Tuṣṭi gave birth to Muda, Puṣṭi gave birth to Smaya, Kriyā gave birth to Yoga, Unnati gave birth to Darpa, Buddhi gave birth to Artha, Medhā gave birth to Smṛti, Titikṣā gave birth to Kṣema, and Hrī gave birth to Praśraya. Mūrti, a reservoir of all respectable qualities, gave birth to Śrī Nara-Nārāyaṇa, the Supreme Personality of Godhead.

verses.sanskrit: ययोर्जन्मन्यदो विश्वमभ्यनन्दत्सुनिर्वृतम् ।मनांसि ककुभो वाता: प्रसेदु: सरितोऽद्रय: ॥ ५३ ॥
verses.transliteration: yayor janmany ado viśvamabhyanandat sunirvṛtammanāṁsi kakubho vātāḥpraseduḥ sarito ’drayaḥ
verses.text: On the occasion of the appearance of Nara-Nārāyaṇa, the entire world was full of joy. Everyone’s mind became tranquil, and thus in all directions the air, the rivers and the mountains became pleasant.

verses.sanskrit: दिव्यवाद्यन्त तूर्याणि पेतु: कुसुमवृष्टय: ।मुनयस्तुष्टुवुस्तुष्टा जगुर्गन्धर्वकिन्नरा: ॥ ५४ ॥नृत्यन्ति स्म स्त्रियो देव्य आसीत्परममङ्गलम् ।देवा ब्रह्मादय: सर्वे उपतस्थुरभिष्टवै: ॥ ५५ ॥
verses.transliteration: divy avādyanta tūryāṇipetuḥ kusuma-vṛṣṭayaḥmunayas tuṣṭuvus tuṣṭājagur gandharva-kinnarāḥ
verses.text: In the heavenly planets, bands began to play, and they showered flowers from the sky. The pacified sages chanted Vedic prayers, the denizens of heaven known as the Gandharvas and Kinnaras sang, the beautiful damsels of the heavenly planets danced, and in this way, at the time of the appearance of Nara-Nārāyaṇa, all signs of good fortune were visible. Just at that time, great demigods like Brahmā also offered their respectful prayers.

verses.sanskrit: देवा ऊचु:यो मायया विरचितं निजयात्मनीदंखे रूपभेदमिव तत्प्रतिचक्षणाय ।एतेन धर्मसदने ऋषिमूर्तिनाद्यप्रादुश्चकार पुरुषाय नम: परस्मै ॥ ५६ ॥
verses.transliteration: devā ūcuḥyo māyayā viracitaṁ nijayātmanīdaṁkhe rūpa-bhedam iva tat-praticakṣaṇāyaetena dharma-sadane ṛṣi-mūrtinādyaprāduścakāra puruṣāya namaḥ parasmai
verses.text: The demigods said: Let us offer our respectful obeisances unto the transcendental Personality of Godhead, who created as His external energy this cosmic manifestation, which is situated in Him as the air and clouds are situated in space, and who has now appeared in the form of Nara-Nārāyaṇa Ṛṣi in the house of Dharma.

verses.sanskrit: सोऽयं स्थितिव्यतिकरोपशमाय सृष्टान्सत्त्वेन न: सुरगणाननुमेयतत्त्व: ।द‍ृश्याददभ्रकरुणेन विलोकनेनयच्छ्रीनिकेतममलं क्षिपतारविन्दम् ॥ ५७ ॥
verses.transliteration: so ’yaṁ sthiti-vyatikaropaśamāya sṛṣṭānsattvena naḥ sura-gaṇān anumeya-tattvaḥdṛśyād adabhra-karuṇena vilokanenayac chrī-niketam amalaṁ kṣipatāravindam
verses.text: Let that Supreme Personality of Godhead, who is understood by truly authorized Vedic literature and who has created peace and prosperity to destroy all calamities of the created world, be kind enough to bestow His glance upon the demigods. His merciful glance can supersede the beauty of the spotless lotus flower which is the home of the goddess of fortune.

verses.sanskrit: एवं सुरगणैस्तात भगवन्तावभिष्टुतौ ।लब्धावलोकैर्ययतुरर्चितौ गन्धमादनम् ॥ ५८ ॥
verses.transliteration: evaṁ sura-gaṇais tātabhagavantāv abhiṣṭutaulabdhāvalokair yayaturarcitau gandhamādanam
verses.text: [Maitreya said:] O Vidura, thus the demigods worshiped with prayers the Supreme Personality of Godhead appearing as the sage Nara-Nārāyaṇa. The Lord glanced upon them with mercy and then departed for Gandhamādana Hill.

verses.sanskrit: ताविमौ वै भगवतो हरेरंशाविहागतौ ।भारव्ययाय च भुव: कृष्णौ यदुकुरूद्वहौ ॥ ५९ ॥
verses.transliteration: tāv imau vai bhagavatoharer aṁśāv ihāgataubhāra-vyayāya ca bhuvaḥkṛṣṇau yadu-kurūdvahau
verses.text: That Nara-Nārāyaṇa Ṛṣi, who is a partial expansion of Kṛṣṇa, has now appeared in the dynasties of Yadu and Kuru, in the forms of Kṛṣṇa and Arjuna respectively, to mitigate the burden of the world.

verses.sanskrit: स्वाहाभिमानिनश्चाग्नेरात्मजांस्त्रीनजीजनत् ।पावकं पवमानं च शुचिं च हुतभोजनम् ॥ ६० ॥
verses.transliteration: svāhābhimāninaś cāgnerātmajāṁs trīn ajījanatpāvakaṁ pavamānaṁ caśuciṁ ca huta-bhojanam
verses.text: The predominating deity of fire begot in his wife, Svāhā, three children, named Pāvaka, Pavamāna and Śuci, who exist by eating the oblations offered to the fire of sacrifice.

verses.sanskrit: तेभ्योऽग्नय: समभवन्चत्वारिंशच्च पञ्च च ।त एवैकोनपञ्चाशत्साकं पितृपितामहै: ॥ ६१ ॥
verses.transliteration: tebhyo ’gnayaḥ samabhavancatvāriṁśac ca pañca cata evaikonapañcāśatsākaṁ pitṛ-pitāmahaiḥ
verses.text: From those three sons another forty-five descendants were generated, who are also fire-gods. The total number of fire-gods is therefore forty-nine, including the fathers and the grandfather.

verses.sanskrit: वैतानिके कर्मणि यन्नामभिर्ब्रह्मवादिभि: ।आग्नेय्य इष्टयो यज्ञे निरूप्यन्तेऽग्नयस्तु ते ॥ ६२ ॥
verses.transliteration: vaitānike karmaṇi yan-nāmabhir brahma-vādibhiḥāgneyya iṣṭayo yajñenirūpyante ’gnayas tu te
verses.text: These forty-nine fire-gods are the beneficiaries of the oblations offered in the Vedic sacrificial fire by impersonalist brāhmaṇas.

verses.sanskrit: अग्निष्वात्ता बर्हिषद: सौम्या: पितर आज्यपा: ।साग्नयोऽनग्नयस्तेषां पत्नी दाक्षायणी स्वधा ॥ ६३ ॥
verses.transliteration: agniṣvāttā barhiṣadaḥsaumyāḥ pitara ājyapāḥsāgnayo ’nagnayas teṣāṁpatnī dākṣāyaṇī svadhā
verses.text: The Agniṣvāttas, the Barhiṣadas, the Saumyas and the Ājyapas are the Pitās. They are either sāgnika or niragnika. The wife of all these Pitās is Svadhā, who is the daughter of King Dakṣa.

verses.sanskrit: तेभ्यो दधार कन्ये द्वे वयुनां धारिणीं स्वधा ।उभे ते ब्रह्मवादिन्यौ ज्ञानविज्ञानपारगे ॥ ६४ ॥
verses.transliteration: tebhyo dadhāra kanye dvevayunāṁ dhāriṇīṁ svadhāubhe te brahma-vādinyaujñāna-vijñāna-pārage
verses.text: Svadhā, who was offered to the Pitās, begot two daughters named Vayunā and Dhāriṇī, both of whom were impersonalists and were expert in transcendental and Vedic knowledge.

verses.sanskrit: भवस्य पत्नी तु सती भवं देवमनुव्रता ।आत्मन: सद‍ृशं पुत्रं न लेभे गुणशीलत: ॥ ६५ ॥
verses.transliteration: bhavasya patnī tu satībhavaṁ devam anuvratāātmanaḥ sadṛśaṁ putraṁna lebhe guṇa-śīlataḥ
verses.text: The sixteenth daughter, whose name was Satī, was the wife of Lord Śiva. She could not produce a child, although she always faithfully engaged in the service of her husband.

verses.sanskrit: पितर्यप्रतिरूपे स्वे भवायानागसे रुषा ।अप्रौढैवात्मनात्मानमजहाद्योगसंयुता ॥ ६६ ॥
verses.transliteration: pitary apratirūpe svebhavāyānāgase ruṣāaprauḍhaivātmanātmānamajahād yoga-saṁyutā
verses.text: The reason is that Satī’s father, Dakṣa, used to rebuke Lord Śiva in spite of Śiva’s faultlessness. Consequently, before attaining a mature age, Satī gave up her body by dint of yogic mystic power.
